diagnosis.final_diagnosis: autosomal dominant progressive external ophthalmoplegia (adPEO) with hypogonadism
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on clinical findings including progressive external ophthalmoplegia (PEO), dysarthria, dysphonia, limb muscle weakness with wasting, absence of Achilles tendon reflexes, and distal vibration sensory loss. Electromyogram (EMG) showed myopathic changes in facial and proximal limb muscles. Muscle biopsies in advanced cases revealed structural abnormalities of mitochondria, ragged-red fibers, and focal cytochrome c oxidase deficiency. The combination of muscle-nerve involvement with PEO, Achilles tendon areflexia, distal vibration sensory impairment, myopathic EMG, and abnormally low sural nerve responses was considered typical of this mitochondrial disorder.
